title: Signals updated at wrong times when using verilator.

features.name: async_reset_flop_with_sync_enable
features.description: Code should include an always block with asynchronous reset and synchronous enable signal that controls when data is captured on clock edge
features.snippet: always @(posedge hclk or negedge hreset_n) if (!hreset_n) foo <= {32{1'b0}}; else if (foo_we) foo <= nxt_foo;

features.name: combinational_next_state_case_statement
features.description: Code should include a combinational always block computing next state via case statement over state machine with multiple states including default with 'x' assignment
features.snippet: always @(...) case(bar_state_i) `BAR_STATE_1: begin nxt_foo = 32'hFFFFFFFF; foo_we = ready; end default: begin nxt_foo = {32{1'bx}}; foo_we = 1'bx; endcase

features.name: feedback_loop_combinational_to_sequential
features.description: Code should include a feedback pattern where combinational logic computes next value (nxt_foo) that feeds a clocked register (foo), creating timing dependency between enable signal and data update
features.snippet: wire [31:0] nxt_foo; assign nxt_foo = some_logic; always @(posedge clk) if (en) foo <= nxt_foo;
